programmes.show_description: It's Jack and May's wedding anniversary, but May is worried that she might be pregnant - meanwhile, Arnold fears that he is about to get the sack. S2 E9: A Pregnant Pause
Yvonne delivers an ultimatum to Gary, but Ron makes him an offer he can't refuse. Will he return to 1942, and if he does, will he ever come back to the 1990s?S1 E6: In the Mood
Mr. Bean wreaks havoc at Harrods when he goes Christmas shopping, then has trouble stuffing a turkey on Christmas Day. E7: Merry Christmas, Mr. Bean

Drama series about a detective who wakes up in what seems to be 1973. As heroin hits the streets of Manchester, CID want culprits for the smuggling, dealing and ensuing violence. With John Simm. S2 E6

Sandra brings home a dog to a flat that doesn't allow pets - Beryl discovers that the landlord is about to do an inspection. Where will the dog go to, and how do they end up with a cat? S2 E9: The Dog
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Pamela is delighted when she receives flowers from the new man in her life, and Malcolm tries to make the most of his mother going away for a weekend. S2 E3: Visiting
Sharon's disapproval of Tracey dating policeman Barry and apparently overlooking Garth's feelings for his dad leads to a major quarrel between the sisters. S12 E3: Tombstone Blues
More mayhem with hapless bumbler Mr. Bean as he struggles to wait in line for an appointment and thinks up some unusual methods to help him sleep. E13: Goodnight Mr. Bean
The last night of the holiday finds both couples packing, but David and Linda decide to slip away and meet for one last time. S2 E7: Adios

Hit '70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, who are forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S5, E3: Barefaced in the Park
Several months have passed - Amy is working, but David is still unemployed and lies to her that his doctor has told him he will have a nervous breakdown unless he takes a holiday. S3 E1: Winter Break
Lord Meldrum holds a musical evening, a royal party is invited to a dinner party, and a surprise invitee is expected to attend later in the evening. S2 E7 Royal Flush
When Miss Jones becomes engaged to a librarian and looks set to move out, Rigsby is distraught but is determined that no other man should have her. S2 E4: Moonlight and Roses
Wolfie and Ken return to Tooting after their stint in prison to find that much has changed in their absence - and not for the better. S4 E2: Changes
Jack and Adrian start their new jobs at the Boating Club, Aveline at the vet's and Ma at the dentist's, while Joey heads for the dole office. S4 E5
The girls are arguing - they need more space and decide to move to a bigger flat, but they need a third person to share the high rent. Can they find anybody? S2 E8: The New Flat
The staff discover that the station is a hundred years old and decide to have a street party to celebrate. S2 E10: Ton Up
Lord Meldrum holds a musical evening, a royal party is invited to a dinner party, and a surprise invitee is expected to attend later in the evening. S2 E7 Royal Flush
When Mildred is going to the Floral Society annual dinner dance but George doesn't want to, Robin agrees to accompany Mildred to the dance and leaves George rather jealous. S6 E3: The Generation Game
Jamila has been arrested for shoplifting. Mr. Brown gathers the class and they set out to return the items without getting caught. That'll be a tricky one. S1 E5: The Best Things in Life
Can our patriotic and unromantic hero be contemplating a trip down under and marriage to Mrs Hollingbery, the woman he loves to hate? It seems so - but there is, of course, an ulterior motive! S4 E1
The girls are arguing - they need more space and decide to move to a bigger flat, but they need a third person to share the high rent. Can they find anybody? S2 E8: The New Flat
Jack and Adrian start their new jobs at the Boating Club, Aveline at the vet's and Ma at the dentist's, while Joey heads for the dole office. S4 E5
Arthur's chum Sidney comes to him for protection from a murderer, but Arthur's idea of a safe house is a caravan belonging to Dave's sister. S10 E3: All Things Brighton Beautiful
The staff discover that the station is a hundred years old and decide to have a street party to celebrate. S2 E10: Ton Up
When Gary needs money for a new house, Ron convinces him to try his investment idea for the second time. S2 E1: Don't Get Around Much Any More
More hilarious mishaps with the bumbling Mr. Bean, this time as he books into a hotel room and makes himself well and truly at home but locks himself out. E8: Mr. Bean in Room 426
Drama series about a detective who wakes up in what seems to be 1973. Sam gets to work on proving DCI Hunt's innocence in the shooting of a local underworld bookie. Starring John Simm. S2 E7
Sandra is convinced into looking after her grandad and gets Beryl to help her, but it seems that grandad is a whole heap of trouble for them both. S2 E10: Grandad
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Malcolm decides to move out when his mother interferes yet again, and David offers information about the enterprise allowance scheme. S2 E4: Visiting II
When Tracey has enough of Dorien entertaining gentlemen callers and Sharon not bringing in a living wage, she throws them both out and they end up in Sharon's old bedsit. S12 E4: Going, Going, Gone
The blunderingly inept master of disaster goes for a haircut - but ends up styling the other customers' hair - and causes chaos at a school fun day. E14: Hair by Mr. Bean of London

Hit '70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, who are forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S5, E4: A Man and a Woman
David is caught out when Amy tells him that Tommy Wright is dead, so he and Linda adopt disguises to avoid each other's spouses as they plan to move to another hotel. S3 E2: Deja Vu
Devious butler Alf chances upon a moneymaking scheme, and Sir Ralph suspects that Lady Agatha is seeing another man and Lord Meldrum is asked to help him. S3 E1 Please Help the Orphans
Rigsby has found a new lodger called Seymour who is staying in town away from his country estate, but Philip and Alan aren't quite as convinced of his authenticity. S2 E5: A Perfect Gentleman
The South African rugby team are arriving on tour, so Wolfie decides to air an anti-fascist protest at an upcoming match. S4 E3: The Final Try
Aveline brings home a sick kestrel, while Roxy arrives on the doorstep with her cat and her belongings. What will turn up next? S4 E6

Sandra brings home a dog to a flat that doesn't allow pets - Beryl discovers that the landlord is about to do an inspection. Where will the dog go to, and how do they end up with a cat? S2 E9: The Dog
The workers of Hatley railway station live a gentle and predictable life, but that is rudely interrupted by some alarming news and a new station master. S1 E1: All Change
Devious butler Alf chances upon a moneymaking scheme, and Sir Ralph suspects that Lady Agatha is seeing another man and Lord Meldrum is asked to help him. S3 E1 Please Help the Orphans
George needs some money and so sells Mildred's sun lamp to Larry; later, when Mildred is upstairs, she sees her lamp and thinks that Robin has stolen it. S6 E4: The Sunshine Boys
It's Mr. Brown's birthday. It's not all happy returns when Ms. Courteney informs Mr. Brown that his trial month is over and she is not satisfied with his teaching. S1 E6: Come Back All Is Forgiven
Arthur is convincing Alf to get married to Mrs Hollingbery. Alf is toying with the idea, not for love, but for the opportunity to put pensions together and get the house cheap off the council! S4 E2

Arthur decides to rent out Bert and Doreen's house while the pair are on holiday, but hasn't realised that his new tenant is in trouble with the police. S10 E4: One Flew Over The Parents Nest
